title: Practice: will, going to, or present continuous?

questions.prompt: The phone is ringing. Complete: "Don't worry — I ___ it!"
questions.explanation: The decision is made at the moment of speaking (spontaneous). Use will: I'll answer it!

questions.prompt: Look at those black clouds! Complete: "It ___ rain."
questions.explanation: The visible evidence (black clouds) calls for "be going to": it is going to rain.

questions.prompt: Complete the time clause: "I will call you when I ___ home."
questions.explanation: After time conjunctions (when, as soon as, before, after, until) in a future sentence, use the present simple — not will: when I get home.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about a plan already decided:

options.text: We will visit Djerba next summer.
We are going to visit Djerba next summer.

questions.explanation: A plan decided before speaking uses "be going to": we are going to visit Djerba next summer.

questions.prompt: Complete: "I ___ the dentist at 3 p.m. tomorrow." (appointment confirmed)
questions.explanation: A confirmed arrangement with a specific time uses the present continuous: I am seeing the dentist at 3 p.m. tomorrow.

questions.prompt: Which time marker does NOT typically go with future tenses?
questions.explanation: "Yesterday" refers to the past, not the future. "Next week", "tomorrow", and "soon" are future time markers.
